betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Rock Crusher§f:
The Rock Crusher (nuclearcraft:rock_crusher) is a single-block machine that grinds rocks and ores into dusts. It is the entry point for many NC rare-earth dusts that don't have direct mining equivalents.

§6Slot Layout (RockCrusherRecipes.java:11)§f:
1 item input → 0 fluid input → 3 item outputs → 0 fluid output. The Rock Crusher produces three byproducts per cycle, each with a configurable chance.

§6Crafting Recipe (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:121)§f:
Pattern "PMP / ACA / PTP" in a 3×3 grid. Letters: P = plateAdvanced, M = motor, A = actuator, C = chassis, T = ingotTough. Yields 1 Rock Crusher.

§6What It Does (RockCrusherRecipes.java:17-30)§f:
The Rock Crusher processes stones into mineral dusts (chance-based):
- §bGranite → Rhodochrosite Dust (40% × 2) + Villiaumite Dust (35%)§f
- §bGranite (alternate mode) → Rhodochrosite Dust (20% × 2) + Villiaumite Dust (35%)§f
- §bDiorite → Zirconium Dust (50% × 2) + Fluorite Dust (45%) + Carobbiite Dust (70%)§f
- §bAndesite → Beryllium Dust (50% × 2) + Alugentum Dust (30%) + Arsenic Dust (30%)§f
- §bAndesite (alternate mode) → Beryllium Dust (25% × 2) + Arsenic Dust (30%)§f

The alternate mode (rock_crusher_alternate) swaps one byproduct for another. Toggle via NCConfig.

§6Stone Variants (CraftingRecipeHandler)§f:
The recipes accept both regular and polished variants:
- stoneGranite, stoneGranitePolished
- stoneDiorite, stoneDioritePolished
- stoneAndesite, stoneAndesitePolished

§6Direct Descendants of Rock Crusher (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 4 items used in the Rock Crusher recipe:
- plateAdvanced (nuclearcraft:part:1)
- motor (nuclearcraft:part:8)
- actuator (nuclearcraft:part:9)
- ingotTough (nuclearcraft:alloy:1)

§6Role in NC Production§f:
The Rock Crusher is the §eentry point for many rare dusts§f that don't have OreDict entries. Stones are essentially unlimited (cobblestone generators, treasure loot, village mason chests), so the Rock Crusher provides a renewable source of:
- §bZirconium Dust§f: used in Zircaloy alloy (reactor cladding)
- §bBeryllium Dust§f: used in BSCCO wire, fission moderators, neutron initiators
- §bFluorite Dust§f: used in Chemical Reactor (hydrofluoric acid)
- §bArsenic Dust§f: used in Chemical Reactor (BAs semiconductor)
- §bAlugentum Dust§f: used in Chemical Reactor (alumina)
- §bRhodochrosite Dust§f: used in alloy production
- §bVilliaumite / Carobbiite Dust§f: rare sodium/potassium fluoride salts

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30021 (Alloy Furnace) — the basic NC unlock.

§eTip§f: For bulk Zirconium production, build a stack of Rock Crushers and feed them cobblestone. Andesite is found in mountain biomes, diorite in taiga/hills, granite near caves.

§eTip§f: Pair the Rock Crusher with an Enricher to convert the produced dusts into solutions, then Crystallizer to recover them as dry dusts (useful for storage).

§cWarning§f: Some Rock Crusher outputs (Arsenic Dust) are toxic. Use proper PPE and avoid carrying them in your inventory.
